title: Veld Project Configuration
description: Configuration file for a Veld local development environment. Place this as veld.json in your project root.

$schema.description: Optional JSON Schema reference for editor autocompletion.

schemaVersion.description: Schema version. Must be "2" for this version of Veld.

name.description: Human-readable project name. Used in URLs and registry entries.

url_template.description: URL template for services. Supports placeholders: {service}, {variant}, {run}, {project}, {branch}, {worktree}, {username}, {hostname}. Supports fallback operator: {branch ?? run}.

presets.description: Named shortcuts for node:variant selections. Each preset maps to an array of "node:variant" strings.

client_log_levels.description: Client-side log levels to capture (project-level default). Overridable at node and variant level.

features.description: Feature toggles (project-level defaults). Overridable at node and variant level.

env.description: Global environment variables inherited by all node variants. Overridable at node and variant level.

sharing.description: Environment-wide sharing policy: which relays to route share traffic through, and the public web gateway URL. Per-service opt-in lives on each variant (share).

setup.description: Steps that run sequentially before the dependency graph executes. If any step exits non-zero, startup is aborted. Not nodes — no variants, no health checks, no dependency graph participation.

teardown.description: Steps that run sequentially after all nodes stop (after per-node on_stop hooks). Best-effort: failures are logged but do not block the stop operation. Commands should be idempotent.

nodes.description: The dependency graph nodes. Each key is a node name, and the value defines its variants.

SetupStep.description: A lightweight step that runs before the dependency graph (setup) or after all nodes stop (teardown). Supports shell environment variables and project-level Veld variables: ${veld.name}, ${veld.project}, ${veld.root}, ${veld.run}.

name.description: Human-readable name for progress reporting and error messages.

command.description: Shell command to execute. Supports ${veld.name}, ${veld.project}, ${veld.root}, ${veld.run}, and shell environment variables.

failureMessage.description: Optional human-friendly message shown when the command fails (non-zero exit). Useful for prerequisite checks like 'Docker must be running'.

NodeConfig.description: Configuration for a single node in the dependency graph.

default_variant.description: The variant to use when none is specified. If omitted and the node has exactly one variant, that variant is used automatically.

url_template.description: URL template override for all variants of this node. Overrides the project-level url_template.

hidden.description: When true, this node is hidden from `veld nodes` output. Hidden nodes still participate in the dependency graph and run normally.

client_log_levels.description: Client-side log levels override for all variants of this node.

features.description: Feature toggles override for all variants of this node.

env.description: Extra environment variables inherited by all variants of this node. Overrides project-level env. Overridable at variant level.

cwd.description: Working directory for all variants of this node. Relative paths are resolved from the project root. Overridable at variant level. Supports Veld variable substitution.

actions.description: User-invokable actions for this node, exposed on the CLI (`veld action <name>`) and as buttons in the management dashboard.

variants.description: Available variants for this node. Each key is a variant name.

VariantConfig.description: Configuration for a single variant of a node.

type.description: Step type. "command" runs a command/script to completion. "start_server" starts a long-running process and waits for it to become healthy.

command.description: Inline shell command to execute. Supports Veld variable substitution (e.g. ${veld.port}, ${node.output}).

script.description: Path to a script file, relative to veld.json. Mutually exclusive with command.

health_check.description: Legacy health check configuration. Deprecated: use probes.readiness instead.

probes.description: Readiness and liveness probe configuration. probes.readiness supersedes the legacy health_check field.

depends_on.description: Dependencies: maps node names to the variant that must be running before this variant starts.

env.description: Extra environment variables injected into the process. Overrides node-level and project-level env.

outputs.description: Output declarations. For command steps: an array of output names captured from $VELD_OUTPUT_FILE (preferred) or VELD_OUTPUT stdout lines (legacy). For start_server steps: an object mapping output names to template strings.

oneOf.description: Declared output names for command steps.
Synthetic output templates for start_server steps.

sensitive_outputs.description: Output keys whose values are sensitive. These are masked in logs and encrypted at rest.

strict_outputs.description: When true (default), fail if the command produces output keys (via $VELD_OUTPUT_FILE or stdout) not declared in "outputs". Set to false to silently ignore undeclared outputs.

skip_if.description: Idempotency check command. Only applies to "command" type variants. If this command exits 0, the step is considered already complete and is skipped. Previously named "verify".

verify.description: Deprecated: use skip_if instead. Alias for skip_if, kept for backward compatibility.

url_template.description: URL template override for this specific variant. Overrides both node-level and project-level url_template.

on_stop.description: Teardown command to run when the environment is stopped. Executed in reverse dependency order. Supports Veld variable substitution.

client_log_levels.description: Client-side log levels override for this specific variant.

features.description: Feature toggles override for this specific variant.

cwd.description: Working directory for this variant. Relative paths are resolved from the project root. Overrides node-level cwd. Supports Veld variable substitution.

share.description: Sharing opt-in for this variant. Absent (or an empty expose list) means the service can never be shared — veld share refuses it.

ActionConfig.description: A user-invokable action attached to a node. Runs a shell command with the node's live outputs available as ${output.KEY} variables and $KEY environment variables, plus the action's static parameters as ${param.KEY}.

name.description: Stable identifier used to invoke the action: `veld action <name>`.

label.description: Human-readable label for the dashboard button. Defaults to the action name.

description.description: Optional one-line description shown in `veld actions` output and as a button tooltip.

command.description: Shell command to execute. Actions are node-scoped: supports ${veld.*}, ${output.KEY} (this node's live outputs), and ${param.KEY} (this action's parameters) substitution. The node's outputs are also injected as environment variables, so $KEY shell references work too. Prefer $KEY over ${output.KEY} for secrets: ${output.DB_PASS} is interpolated into the command string (visible in the process list), whereas $DB_PASS is expanded by the shell at runtime and never appears in argv.

parameters.description: Static key/value parameters baked into the action. Available to the command as ${param.KEY} and as $KEY environment variables. Values support ${veld.*} and ${output.KEY} substitution.

requires_outputs.description: Output keys that must all be present on the running node for this action to be available. Gates both CLI invocation and dashboard button visibility. When omitted, the action is always available for a running node.

ProbesConfig.description: Readiness and liveness probe configuration. Available for both command and start_server variants.

readiness.description: Readiness probe — gates the dependency graph during startup. For start_server nodes: checked after process starts. For command nodes: checked after command exits 0.

liveness.description: Liveness probe — runs continuously after the node is healthy. Triggers recovery when failure_threshold consecutive checks fail.

LivenessProbe.description: Liveness probe configuration. Runs periodically after a node becomes healthy to detect failures (e.g., dropped SSH tunnels, crashed background processes).

type.description: Liveness check strategy. "http" polls an HTTP endpoint. "port" checks if the port is accepting connections. "command" runs a command and checks the exit code.

expect_status.description: Expected HTTP status code (type "http" only). Defaults to 200.

command.description: Shell command to run (type "command" only). Exit code 0 means healthy.

failure_threshold.description: Number of consecutive failures before triggering recovery.

max_recoveries.description: Maximum number of recovery attempts before the node is permanently marked as failed.

FeaturesConfig.description: Feature toggles for controlling which Veld capabilities are injected into a server's HTML responses. All properties are optional — omitted values inherit from the parent level (variant inherits from node, node from project). The built-in default for all features is true (enabled).

feedback_overlay.description: Inject the feedback overlay toolbar (FAB, screenshot, comments) into HTML responses. Default: true.

client_logs.description: Inject the client-side log collector into HTML responses. Default: true.

inject.description: Automatically inject bootstrap scripts into HTML responses. When false, the /__veld__/* proxy routes are still created so you can manually add <script> tags. Default: true.

ClientLogLevels.description: Client-side console log levels to capture. Supported values: "log", "warn", "error", "info", "debug". Unhandled exceptions ("exception") are always captured regardless of this setting.

EnvMap.description: A map of environment variable names to values. Values support Veld variable substitution (e.g. ${veld.port}, ${nodes.backend.url}).

relays.description: Which iroh relays to route share traffic through. Must be opted into explicitly (no implicit default) — veld share is refused if unset and no VELD_SHARE_RELAY env override is present. "public" uses n0's public relays; an array confines traffic to self-hosted relays. Each array entry is either a bare relay URL string or a { url, token } object; the optional token gates a self-hosted relay via an Authorization: Bearer header. When set, config wins over the VELD_SHARE_RELAY env override.

gateway.description: The public web gateway this environment registers web shares with (used by `veld share --web`). Either a bare base-URL string (e.g. https://share.acme.internal), or an object carrying the gateway's registration auth token. Only needed for services that expose "web".

oneOf.description: Gateway base URL (auth token supplied via the VELD_SHARE_GATEWAY_TOKEN env var on the daemon).

token.description: Registration auth token presented to the gateway as an Authorization: Bearer header. Resolved on the daemon when the web share starts; a token that fails to resolve fails the share.

dangerouslyEmbedRelayTokensInTicket.description: DANGER. When true, the resolved relay auth token(s) are embedded in the share ticket so a joiner needs no out-of-band token config. This puts the relay secret into every share link (Slack, email, browser history) — only enable for disposable, per-project tokens you rotate freely, never a shared org relay secret. Off by default; the join side otherwise prompts for the token and caches it locally. camelCase (à la React's dangerouslySetInnerHTML) to stand out as a deliberate, dangerous choice.

RelayEntry.description: A self-hosted relay in a sharing.relays list: either a bare URL string, or an object with a url and an optional authorization token.

token.description: Authorization token sent to the relay as an Authorization: Bearer header, gating who may use a self-hosted relay. Resolved on the daemon at share time; a token that fails to resolve fails the share.

SecretSource.description: Where a secret is read from at use time. A plain string is a literal (inline in config, so it lands in version control — prefer the object forms). Object forms keep the secret out of config: env reads an environment variable; file reads a file (Docker/K8s secret mounts); command runs a shell command and uses its stdout (1Password/Vault CLI).

oneOf.description: Literal secret value, inline in config.

env.description: Name of an environment variable (read from the daemon's process environment) holding the secret.

file.description: Path to a file whose contents (trailing whitespace trimmed) are the secret.

command.description: Shell command (run via sh -c) whose stdout (trailing whitespace trimmed) is the secret.

expose.description: Audiences this service may be exposed to. Empty means not shareable. "peer" = other Veld users via `veld share` (verbatim URL). "web" = any browser, no Veld needed, via `veld share --web` and the sharing.gateway (real public URL, best-effort fidelity).

web.description: Web-audience settings for this service's public URL.

access.description: Viewer access control. "password" (the default, also when absent): the gateway requires the share password — generated and printed by `veld share --web` — before serving. "link": anyone with the URL is served; the unguessable slug is the only gate. An explicit value here always wins over the `--access` CLI flag.

type.description: Health check strategy. "http" polls an HTTP endpoint. "port" checks if the port is accepting connections. "command" runs a command and checks the exit code.

expect_status.description: Expected HTTP status code (type "http" only). Defaults to 200.

command.description: Shell command to run (type "command" only). Exit code 0 means healthy.

timeout_seconds.description: Maximum seconds to wait for the service to become healthy.
